nativeRange.habitat: Cryptic reef-dweller — generally found in the lower littoral zone and deeper reef areas, under boulders and amongst coral and rubble.

diet.notes: Largely a scavenger and detritivore — picks through the sand bed and rockwork for uneaten food, detritus, and small organic particles. In aquaria, supplemental feeding of small pieces of meaty food (mysis, brine shrimp, finely chopped seafood) two to three times per week is generally advised, ideally placed near or directly under the star during the evening when it emerges to feed.

avoidWith: predatory wrasses that pick at invertebrates
puffers and triggers that may bite arms
very small or sleeping fish placed within reach (opportunistic capture has been reported)

summary: An Indo-Pacific brittle star with cream-and-dark banded smooth arms. Generally considered one of the easier serpent stars — a nocturnal detritivore that is reef safe and helps stir the sand bed.
careNotes: The Banded Serpent Star is widely considered one of the easier brittle/serpent stars to keep, valued for its striking cream-and-dark banded arms (often described as tiger-striped) and its quiet scavenging behavior. Unlike the bristly Ophiocoma species, its arms are smooth, and it tends to remain hidden in rockwork or under sand by day, emerging at night to forage. Trade naming is inconsistent — LiveAquaria has listed it under multiple SKUs and sometimes mislabels the origin as Caribbean, but the species (Ophiolepis superba H.L. Clark, 1915) is in fact Indo-Pacific, ranging from the Red Sea and East Africa through the Indo-West Pacific to North Australia and Japan, generally to about 50 m depth. Like all echinoderms it is highly sensitive to changes in salinity, pH, and dissolved oxygen, and is intolerant of copper-based medications — slow drip acclimation over at least an hour is generally advised, and the species is best added only to a mature, stable tank (commonly cited as at least 3–6 months established). Sources broadly agree on water parameters (around 23–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025) and on reef safety; reported minimum tank sizes vary from about 75 L (20 US gal) at the low end up to 200 L on more cautious sources, so 100 L is used here as a sensible floor given that mature specimens can span 20–30 cm tip-to-tip. Lifespan reports range from 3–8 years in most aquaria up to anecdotal claims of decades; the conservative 3–8 year range is used. Although generally peaceful, brittle stars are opportunistic and may capture small or compromised fish that come within reach — keep this in mind for very small or weakened tankmates.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding of Ophiolepis superba is not commonly reported and is generally considered impractical for the home aquarist. Like other ophiuroids it has separate sexes and a planktonic larval stage that is difficult to rear in closed systems; specimens in trade are wild-collected.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: The originally specified LiveAquaria URL 404'd; substituted the Caribbean Serpent Sea Star SKU page which carries the standard LiveAquaria water-parameter and care boilerplate for serpent stars (temp 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, dKH 8–12). Note that LiveAquaria mislabels the origin as Caribbean — Ophiolepis superba is in fact Indo-Pacific (see WoRMS/Reeflings).

additional.name: Reef2Reef Wiki
additional.notes: Secondary source from the manifest. The wiki root returned 403 on direct fetch; cross-verification was performed against multiple alternative secondaries instead.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name, peaceful temperament, reef safety, adult max ~20 cm, minimum tank 200 L, temperature ~26°C, SG ~1.026. Listed range as West Indian Ocean / Red Sea (consistent with Indo-Pacific distribution).

additional.name: Reeflings
additional.notes: Confirmed family Ophiolepididae, Indo-Pacific range, detritivore diet, reef-safe, peaceful temperament, minimum tank 80 L, temperature 24–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.026, adult size up to 30 cm tip-to-tip.

additional.name: Aquariumbreeder (Shrimp and Snail Breeder)
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size 15–20 cm, temperature 22–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, lifespan typically 3–5 years up to ~8 years, minimum tank 20 US gal (~76 L), care level easy to medium.

additional.name: WoRMS / GBIF (taxonomy)
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomic authority: Ophiolepis superba H.L. Clark, 1915; family Ophiolepididae; order Ophiurida; documented Indo-Pacific range from Red Sea to North Australia, depth to ~50 m.
